strengths: Roughly 19 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded and high-throughput backend systems, clearing the 15-year minimum
Founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes, showing entrepreneurial initiative and long-term product ownership
Consistent record of knowledge-sharing — conference talks, multi-day courses and workshops — relevant to the responsibility of inspiring others internally and externally
concerns: No Director- or Principal-level track record: career has stayed at Senior/Staff IC delivery roles, with no evidence of organization-wide technical strategy, advising strategic customers, or steering implementation strategy across teams

AI credentials are thin for an AI-strategy role: AI exposure is limited to roughly one year of agent-tooling/MCP work at re:cinq, with no demonstrated AI products or platforms of record and no recognized AI expertise (talks cover GIS, testing and web internals, not AI)
No advanced degree in an AI-related field — the preferred Master's/PhD is absent, and formal training is a CS bachelor's plus a fine-arts degree, with no ML/AI education or research background

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs Director-level strategy role
Limited AI depth and external recognition
Breadth and length of engineering experience

response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile senior engineer, but the role demands a recognized AI strategist operating as a Director-level IC — identifying the future state of cloud products, advising strategic customers, and leading disruptive initiatives. His resume shows hands-on delivery at Senior/Staff level with no strategy, advisory or organizational-leadership evidence; his AI experience is recent, narrow and tooling-focused rather than a body of demonstrated AI products or acknowledged expertise; and cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is incidental to product work rather than platform strategy. Measured against the core of this specification, he is a weak fit whose strengths sit one to two levels below the role's demands.
